---
title: "Getting Started"
sidebar: en
---

## Requirements

- A self-hosted, reachable SeaTable instance (HTTPS recommended).
- An API token (workspace- or base-level — see below).

## Create an API token in SeaTable

SeaTable offers two API-token tiers — both work with the app:

| Tier | Where to create | Scope |
|------|-----------------|-------|
| **Workspace API token** | Workspace → Settings → API tokens | access to every base in the workspace |
| **Base API token** | Open the base → ⋯ menu → API token | access to this single base only |

Either way: copy the token, paste it into the app.

## Configure the app

1. Install the app from the store and open it.
2. In the setup dialog, enter:
   - **Server URL** (e.g. `https://seatable.example.org`)
   - **API Token** from the step above
3. The app exchanges the API token for a short-lived base token and loads the list of reachable bases.

![Setup screen with fields for server URL and API token.](../_screens/android-login.png){width=320}

## Notes

- The base token expires regularly; the app fetches a fresh one in the background — no re-entry.
- The app edits rows directly via the SeaTable API; bigger schema changes (new columns, new tables) stay in the web UI.
- Credentials remain in the platform secure store.
